# Sara Foundation Africa

> Non-profit empowering African youth and women with tech skills, mentorship, and career development across 35+ universities in 8 countries.

Sara Foundation runs flagship programs (CAP, FLIP, GJP) that combine intensive learning, mentorship, and real-world projects to prepare African students for tech careers.

## Pages

- [Home](/): Mission overview, programs, impact, and partners.
- [About](/about): Our story, leadership team, and SDG alignment.
- [Contact](/contact): Email, phone, and office locations.
- [Donate](/donation): Support our mission via Naira transfer, crypto, or GoFundMe.
- [Blog](/blog): Articles on African tech education, women in tech, and graduate outcomes.

## Programs

- [CAP Tech Hub](/programs/cap): Career Advancement Program — 13-week intensive (2 weeks learning, 10 weeks build, 1 week launch) and 6-week Fellowship paths.
- [CAP Apply](/programs/cap/apply): Application form for CAP cohort.
- [FLIP](/programs/flip): Female Leadership Initiative Program — mentorship and workshops for women in tech.
- [FLIP Apply](/programs/flip/apply): Application form for FLIP fellowship.
- [GJP](/programs/gjp): Graduate Jobs Program connecting graduates to tech roles.
- [GJP Apply](/programs/gjp/apply): Application form for GJP.

## Partnership

- [Partnership Overview](/partnership): Ways to partner with Sara Foundation.
- [Schools & Communities](/partnership/school-community): University and community partnerships.
- [Organizations](/partnership/organizations): Corporate and NGO partnerships.
- [Sponsors](/partnership/sponsors): Sponsorship opportunities.
